step_by_step_thinking:
Agree.

The Km value indeed primarily indicates the substrate concentration at which the reaction rate reaches half of its maximum velocity (half maximal velocity), reflecting the strength of the enzyme-substrate interaction. This measure directly correlates with the affinity of the enzyme for its substrate—a key aspect often overlooked yet crucial in many physiological contexts. Therefore, option C accurately captures the essence of what the Km value signifies in enzyme kinetics. My final answer remains consistent with the collective agreement reached earlier.
